contract.goal: Ship plugins/linear-loom/cli/lib/plan-parser.ts — the PLAN.md → tree parser per DESIGN.md § 12.1, § 12.2, § 12.6. Pure markdown-to-tree transformation; no Linear-side I/O. U2/U3 will compose this against Linear's current state during diff + apply.
acceptance_criteria: parsePlan(markdown) → {phases: ParsedPhase[]}. ParsedPhase carries {kind:'phase', number, id, was, composed_key, prose, body, batches[]}. ParsedBatch + ParsedTask are parallel.
Composed keys per § 12.2: <phase-id> for phases, <phase-id>.<batch-id> for batches, <phase-id>.<batch-id>.<task-id> for tasks.
Only the ## Phases section parsed; other ## sections (Context, Risks, Decisions log) ignored.
plan-parse-failed thrown for: missing ## Phases, multiple ## Phases, Task outside Batch, duplicate IDs at any parent level, invalid kebab-case ID.
was=<old-id> annotation supported on Phase, Batch, Task (§ 12.6).
Em-dash (—) and hyphen (-) tolerated as heading separator.
Phase + Batch prose-body lines captured in node's body field.
flattenPlan walks the tree in document order, emitting FlatNode per node with composed_key + kind.
rules_applied: DESIGN.md § 12.1 (subheading scheme: ## Phases wrapper, ### Phase N [id] — prose, #### Batch N [id] — prose, - [id] prose).
Pure function: no I/O, no globals, no Linear API calls. Fully testable without mocking.
disqualifiers: Reading from disk (caller supplies the markdown string).
Conflating composed-key uniqueness with global-uniqueness (parent-scoped only).
inputs: PLAN.md Phase 5 D1 (the parser is the first generate-flow deliverable)

actions: Wrote plugins/linear-loom/cli/lib/plan-parser.ts (~225 lines): ParsedPlan / ParsedPhase / ParsedBatch / ParsedTask types, parsePlan function (extractPhasesSection → header-by-header walk → duplicate-ID checks), flattenPlan + FlatNode helper for the U2/U3 diff step.

Regex grammar: ID_BRACKET matches `[<kebab>]` or `[<kebab> was=<kebab>]`; PHASE_HEADING / BATCH_HEADING tolerate em-dash and hyphen; TASK_BULLET matches `- [<id>] <prose>`. kebab-case ID validation enforced at parse time (no uppercase, no leading/trailing dash).

Wrote 15 unit tests: happy path with composed-key assertions, ignored ## sections, Task-outside-Batch error, missing-Phases error, every duplicate-ID variant (Phase / Batch under same Phase / Task under same Batch), parent-scoped same-ID-allowed positive case, was= annotation on all three node kinds, three invalid-ID rejection paths, multiple-Phases error, em-dash/hyphen tolerance, body-prose capture under Phase + Batch, flattenPlan document-order walk, LinearLoomError code check.
Verified: plan-parser tests → 15/15. Full repo suite → 768 passing (was 753 after Phase 4; +15).

Panel: all 4 evaluators approved. test-unit raised a non-blocking advisory about the catch-block test lacking expect.assertions(2); fixed mid-iteration with a 1-line addition so a future SUT change that stops throwing wouldn't silently pass.
corrections: Test-unit advisory addressed mid-iteration: added `expect.assertions(2)` to the catch-block test on LinearLoomError code so a future SUT change that stops throwing wouldn't silently pass green.

changes_since_previous: First unit on the ev-agent.linear-loom.tasks-parser branch, cut from main after PR #40 closed Phase 4. This unit is the start of Phase 5 (Plan-to-tasks generation), per PLAN.md the most load-bearing verb in linear-loom — Sub-Issue reconciliation against PLAN.md as source of truth.
notes_for_pr: Parser is the lookup-end of the Phase 5 generate flow — U2/U3 fetch Linear state, diff against this tree, apply create/update operations. U4 writes Linear URLs back into PLAN.md after generation.

Composed-key scheme is the substrate identity primitive for everything Linear-side. A Sub-Issue's stable identity is its composed key (e.g. `design-1.skeleton-1.decisions-1`); the U2/U3 diff matches existing Linear items by composed key, not by title. Rename detection via the `was=<old-id>` annotation re-keys Linear records on parse (the diff step preserves Linear-side state across an ID change).

Naming-evaluator observation worth a future revisit (non-blocking): `numberStr` is a local variable name that matches the Hungarian shape (Str suffix on a value about to be parseInt'd). Sibling local `idRaw` uses the `Raw` prefix convention for the same idea. Unifying them would help, but neither obscures meaning and both are file-local. Punted to a future revision.

Substrate-followup candidate (also non-blocking, naming-evaluator-surfaced): `prose` (one-line heading text) vs `body` (multi-line continuation) is structurally clear but a reader scanning the type definitions has to read the parser to understand the relationship. `headingProse + bodyProse` or `summary + body` would be more self-describing. Worth considering for Phase 5/6 polish.
Tests are pure-function shape: each test feeds a literal markdown string fixture and asserts on the parsed structure. No mocks at all — the SUT has no dependencies.
